description_long: This object consists of a group of six lotion containers, each with a distinct cylindrical or tube-like shape, arranged in a row. The containers vary in size and design, with some featuring rounded caps and others having flat or slightly angled tops. The surfaces are predominantly smooth and appear to be made of plastic, with a few containers displaying subtle label markings or minimalistic graphics. The color palette is mostly white and light gray, with occasional hints of green or blue on some labels. The containers are typical of personal care products found in urban environments, such as on sidewalks, in parks, or near public restrooms. Each container is likely designed to dispense lotion through a squeeze or flip-top mechanism.
description: A group of six smooth, white and gray lotion containers of varying shapes and sizes, featuring minimalistic labels and caps.
description_view_0: Front view showing all six lotion containers lined up, with visible label markings and cap styles.
description_view_1: Side view of a single, tall, pointed lotion container, highlighting its narrow profile and cap.
description_view_2: Rear view of the group, showing the backs of the containers with less visible labeling and more focus on the container shapes.
description_view_3: Opposite side view of the pointed container, emphasizing its symmetry and the details of the cap.
